subject: Sociology
title: Socialization
description: A fast breakdown of Socialization for builders.

hook.text: A child raised by wolves can't speak or use a fork. Being human isn't automatic — it's learned from other humans.

buildup.text: Socialization is the lifelong process of learning norms, values, and behaviors expected by your society.

discovery.text: Family, schools, peers, and media all act as agents of socialization, each shaping different parts of identity.

twist.text: Socialization doesn't end in childhood. New jobs, prisons, and military service all resocialize adults dramatically.

climax.text: Without socialization, there is no self. The person you think you are was assembled by every group you belonged to.

punchline.text: You weren't born you. Society built you, piece by piece.

quiz.question: What is socialization?
options: The lifelong process of learning social norms and values
A type of political campaign strategy
The act of attending social events
